REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P., CHINA LIVE, LLC DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR CROSS-COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING, SO THAT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT DUPLICATIVE OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEMURRER IS ALSO SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSES OF ACTION TO ALLEGE AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY REGARDING FUTURE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE PARTIES THAT ARE NOT ADDRESSING THE PAST WRONGS (BREACH OF CONTRACT) AND WILL NOT BE NECESSARILY ADDRESSED IN THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT (E.G. IN ORDER TO ESTABLISH THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINTANT BREACHED A CONTRACTUAL PROVISION TO ALLOW ACCESS TO THE BOOKS, THE TRIER OF FACT WILL HAVE TO DETERMINE THAT SUCH CONTRACTUAL PROVISION EXISTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS A RIGHT TO ACCESS THE BOOKS). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Motion For An Award Of Attorneys' Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Daniel L. Goodkin And Lisa Hiraide In Support
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Lisa Hiraide, Esq. for Plaintiff China Live Ventures Limited, L.P.

No hearing held.

The court is unable to hear any arguments as Plaintiff's notice to prevailing party is untimely.

The court adopts the tentative ruling as modified;

INTERVENOR GREGG WILLIAMS Motion For An Award Of Attorneys' Fees;

is DENIED without prejudice. It is impossible to determine the lodestar amount (the number of hours reasonably expended multiplied by the reasonable hourly rate) for fees requested and for costs requested based on the papers submitted to the Court. The total amount of costs requested and why each cost / each category of costs are recoverable is not stated anywhere in the motion papers or the proposed order. Different numbers are listed in the moving papers for fees alone and for fees and costs combined. Costs are not included in the proposed order, but "expenses" appear to be included in the totals requested for fees.
Compare:
a. Notice of Motion: $586,984.47 in attorneys' fees in [sic] costs.
b. Points and Authorities (2: 4-5; 4:9-10) - $501,145.72 in attorney's fees and costs incurred, with additional fees to be awarded for necessary post-judgment enforcement proceedings.
c. Declaration on Daniel Goodkin - total amount of attorneys' fees incurred in this matter amount of $501,145.72 "as reflected in Exhibit A and Exhibit B".
d. Points and Authorities (5:7-8) - The total fees incurred to date are $556,052.63, exclusive of additional fees that will be reasonably incurred for post-judgment enforcement and collection efforts.
e. Proposed Order - $518,546.72 in total fees, including anticipated fees of $5,000 for this motion, reply and hearing.
f. Conclusion section asks for $561,052.63 in total fees and costs.
Exhibit A is a compilation of invoices from 7/15/2024 to 6/15/2025. The some total of these invoices (containing fees and expenses) is $501,145.72 . There are 6 timekeepers identified in the invoices. Only two timekeepers are identified in the supporting declarations.
Exhibit B is a document titled Time & Fees listing (unredacted) entries from July 1, 2025 to August 21, 2025. No total is provided for these entries, but all of them are identified as "billable" by three timekeepers. One of such "billable" entries is identified as "TEST." (7/02/25). Many of the entries contain no description of work performed beyond "services provided on [date certain]). There appear to be no expenses listed. The total for unredacted entries from July 1, 2025 to August 21, 2025 not yet reduced to an invoice as of October 24, 2025 appears to be $14,041.50.
The sum of amounts identified in Exhibits A and B for fees and expenses is $515,187.22 . Expenses, as listed in Exhibit A (no expenses identified in Exhibit B), total $ 59,121.98. According to the proposed order the amount of fees alone per Ex. A and B is $513,546.72. Proposed Order (2:1-2) "$513,546.72 for fees incurred from July 15, 2024 through August 31, 2025 (as documented in Exhibits A and B)." There is no explanation for the $513,546.72 number. There is also no explanation for any other amounts referenced in the Notice of Motion, various places in the points and authorities, body of the declarations and the proposed order.
Therefore, having not been able to determine based on the evidence presented that the moving party is entitled to the attorney's fees and costs in the amount requested per the Notice of Motion, $586,984.47, the motion for fees and costs is DENIED without prejudice.
